# ~/.bashrc: executed by bash(1) for non-login shells.

# If not running interactively, don't do anything
case $- in
    *i*) ;;
      *) return;;
esac

# don't put duplicate lines or lines starting with space in the history.
HISTCONTROL=ignoreboth

# append to the history file, don't overwrite it
shopt -s histappend

# for setting history length see HISTSIZE and HISTFILESIZE in bash(1)
HISTSIZE=1000
HISTFILESIZE=2000

# check the window size after each command and, if necessary,
# update the values of LINES and COLUMNS.
shopt -s checkwinsize

# make less more friendly for non-text input files, see lesspipe(1)
[ -x /usr/bin/lesspipe ] && eval "$(SHELL=/bin/sh lesspipe)"

# set variable identifying the chroot you work in (used in the prompt below)
if [ -z "${debian_chroot:-}" ] && [ -r /etc/debian_chroot ]; then
    debian_chroot=$(cat /etc/debian_chroot)
fi

# set a fancy prompt
case "$TERM" in
    xterm-color|*-256color) color_prompt=yes;;
esac

if [ "$color_prompt" = yes ]; then
    PS1='${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\[\033[01;32m\]\u@\h\[\033[00m\]:\[\033[01;34m\]\w\[\033[00m\]\$ '
else
    PS1='${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\u@\h:\w\$ '
fi
unset color_prompt force_color_prompt

# If this is an xterm set the title to user@host:dir
case "$TERM" in
xterm*|rxvt*)
    PS1="\[\e]0;${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\u@\h: \w\a\]$PS1"
    ;;
esac

# enable color support of ls and also add handy aliases
if [ -x /usr/bin/dircolors ]; then
    test -r ~/.dircolors && eval "$(dircolors -b ~/.dircolors)" || eval "$(dircolors -b)"
    alias ls='ls --color=auto'
    alias grep='grep --color=auto'
    alias fgrep='fgrep --color=auto'
    alias egrep='egrep --color=auto'
fi

# some more ls aliases
alias ll='ls -alF'
alias la='ls -A'
alias l='ls -CF'

# Alias definitions.
if [ -f ~/.bash_aliases ]; then
    . ~/.bash_aliases
fi

# enable programmable completion features
if ! shopt -oq posix; then
  if [ -f /usr/share/bash-completion/bash_completion ]; then
    . /usr/share/bash-completion/bash_completion
  elif [ -f /etc/bash_completion ]; then
    . /etc/bash_completion
  fi
fi

# Standard PATHs
export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# NVM (Node Version Manager)
export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"

# Cargo (Rust)
[ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ] && . "$HOME/.cargo/env"

# opencode
export PATH=/home/mega/.opencode/bin:$PATH

# The OmegA Sovereign Monogram (Yettragrammaton)
if [ -x /home/mega/bin/yettragrammaton.py ]; then
    /home/mega/bin/yettragrammaton.py
else
    # Fallback if script is missing
    echo -e "\e[1;36m       (~)____     \e[1;33m _  _   \e[1;36m   (~)___ "
    echo -e "\e[1;36m        |_| ) )   \e[1;33m / \/ \  \e[1;36m   |_| | "
    echo -e "\e[1;36m        | |/ /   \e[1;33m / /  \ \ \e[1;36m   | | | "
    echo -e "\e[1;36m        |  _/    \e[1;33m| | /\ | |\e[1;36m   | | | "
    echo -e "\e[1;36m        | |\ \   \e[1;33m| |(__)| |\e[1;36m   | \_| "
    echo -e "\e[1;36m        |_| \_\   \e[1;33m\ \__/ / \e[1;36m    (____)"
    echo -e "\e[1;33m                   \____/  \e[1;36m    (___) "
    echo -e "\e[0m"
fi

# --- Gemini CLI Operational Economy (Optimized by Gemini CLI) ---
export GEMINI_API_KEY="AIzaSyCjRvRl8xVWbUJncshZoO9QxAyguMGD2yg"

# Global System Instruction for Token Efficiency (Applies to ALL models)
export GEMINI_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION="You are a token-efficient engineering assistant. Provide high-signal, concise responses. Avoid conversational filler. Prioritize surgical code edits over re-printing entire files."

# Internal helper to launch gemini with operational best practices
_g_exec() {
    local model_env=$1
    shift
    # Operational Rule: Always attempt to resume if no prompt is provided, 
    # but warn if the session is becoming 'heavy' (future implementation)
    if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
        echo -e "\e[1;32m[Economy Mode] Resuming latest session for efficiency...\e[0m"
        eval "$model_env gemini -r latest"
    else
        eval "$model_env gemini \"$@\""
    fi
}

gsmart() { _g_exec "GEMINI_API_KEY=\"\"" "$@"; }
gflash() { _g_exec "GEMINI_MODEL=\"gemini-3-flash-preview\" GEMINI_API_KEY=\"AIzaSyCjRvRl8xVWbUJncshZoO9QxAyguMGD2yg\"" "$@"; }
glite() { _g_exec "GEMINI_MODEL=\"gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview\" GEMINI_API_KEY=\"AIzaSyCjRvRl8xVWbUJncshZoO9QxAyguMGD2yg\"" "$@"; }
gpro() { _g_exec "GEMINI_MODEL=\"gemini-3.1-pro-preview\" GEMINI_API_KEY=\"AIzaSyCjRvRl8xVWbUJncshZoO9QxAyguMGD2yg\"" "$@"; }

# Maintenance: List and clean old sessions to keep the context-loader fast
gclean() {
    echo -e "\e[1;33m[Economy Mode] Current sessions for this project:\e[0m"
    gemini --list-sessions
    echo -e "\e[1;31mUsage: gemini --delete-session <index>\e[0m"
}

# Forced Compression: Quickly shrink the latest session's context window
gcompress() {
    echo -e "\e[1;34m[Economy Mode] Compressing latest session history...\e[0m"
    gemini -r latest -p "/compress"
}

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
import time
import math
import argparse
import signal

# --- Configuration ---
# The Sovereign Palette: Cyan -> Deep Blue -> Purple -> Magenta -> Gold -> Orange -> Red -> Back to Cyan
# This creates a "Royal/Cosmic" flow that bridges the "OmegA" (Blue/Cyan) and "Sovereign" (Gold/Red) themes.
GRADIENT_STOPS = [
    (0,   255, 255), # Cyan
    (0,   120, 255), # Dodger Blue
    (138, 43,  226), # Blue Violet
    (255, 0,   255), # Magenta
    (255, 215, 0),   # Gold
    (255, 140, 0),   # Dark Orange
    (255, 69,  0),   # Red Orange
    (0,   255, 255)  # Loop back to Cyan
]

# Color Constants
C_GREY = "\033[1;30m"
C_RESET = "\033[0m"

BANNER_LINES = [
    "    ██████╗     ██╗    ██╗    ███████╗    ██╗   ██╗",
    "    ██╔══██╗    ██║    ██║    ██╔════╝    ╚██╗ ██╔╝",
    "    ██████╔╝    ██║ █╗ ██║    █████╗       ╚████╔╝ ",
    "    ██╔══██╗    ██║███╗██║    ██╔══╝        ╚██╔╝  ",
    "    ██║  ██║    ╚███╔███╔╝    ██║            ██║   ",
    "    ╚═╝  ╚═╝     ╚══╝╚══╝     ╚═╝            ╚═╝   "
]

SUBTITLE_1 = "THE OMEGA SOVEREIGN MONOGRAM"
SUBTITLE_2 = "(YETTRAGRAMMATON)"

# --- Color Engine ---

def interpolate_color(color1, color2, t):
    """Linear interpolation between two RGB colors."""
    r1, g1, b1 = color1
    r2, g2, b2 = color2
    return (
        int(r1 + (r2 - r1) * t),
        int(g1 + (g2 - g1) * t),
        int(b1 + (b2 - b1) * t)
    )

def get_gradient_color(progress):
    """
    Get color at a specific progress point (0.0 to 1.0) in the multi-stop gradient.
    Handles wrapping automatically.
    """
    progress = progress % 1.0
    num_stops = len(GRADIENT_STOPS) - 1
    segment_length = 1.0 / num_stops
    
    segment_index = int(progress / segment_length)
    segment_progress = (progress - (segment_index * segment_length)) / segment_length
    
    # Safety clamp
    segment_index = min(segment_index, num_stops - 1)
    
    return interpolate_color(
        GRADIENT_STOPS[segment_index],
        GRADIENT_STOPS[segment_index + 1],
        segment_progress
    )

def ansi_rgb(r, g, b):
    return f"\033[38;2;{r};{g};{b}m"

def reset_code():
    return "\033[0m"

# --- Rendering ---

def render_line(text, y_index, width, time_offset, spread=0.03):
    """
    Renders a single line where color shifts based on X position and Time.
    spread: how 'tight' the rainbow is. Higher = more colors visible at once.
    """
    rendered = ""
    # Ghostly color for the solid "body" of the letters
    # Using a very dark grey (almost black) for that "phantom" look
    r_ghost, g_ghost, b_ghost = (40, 45, 50) 
    
    for x, char in enumerate(text):
        if char == " ":
            rendered += char
            continue
            
        # Logic: Solid blocks are the "Ghostly Body"
        # Lines/Corners are the "Glowing Outline/Shadow"
        if char in ['█']:
             rendered += ansi_rgb(r_ghost, g_ghost, b_ghost) + char
        else:
             # Gradient logic for outlines
             wave_pos = (x * spread) + (y_index * 0.05) + time_offset
             r, g, b = get_gradient_color(wave_pos)
             rendered += ansi_rgb(r, g, b) + char
             
    return rendered + reset_code()

def print_banner(time_offset=0.0):
    output = []
    
    # Main Block
    width = len(BANNER_LINES[0])
    for i, line in enumerate(BANNER_LINES):
        output.append(render_line(line, i, width, time_offset))
    
    # Spacing
    output.append("")
    
    # Subtitles (Centered-ish)
    # We apply a slower, broader gradient to the subtitle for elegance
    sub_offset = time_offset + 0.5 # Shift phase
    
    sub1_padding = " " * 6
    sub1_colored = render_line(SUBTITLE_1, len(BANNER_LINES)+1, len(SUBTITLE_1), sub_offset, spread=0.01)
    output.append(f"{sub1_padding}{sub1_colored}")
    
    sub2_padding = " " * 11
    sub2_colored = render_line(SUBTITLE_2, len(BANNER_LINES)+2, len(SUBTITLE_2), sub_offset, spread=0.01)
    output.append(f"{sub2_padding}{sub2_colored}")
    
    return "\n".join(output)

# --- Modes ---

def run_once():
    # Use current time to generate a unique snapshot every time it runs
    # This makes the shell startup feel "alive" without blocking
    now = time.time()
    # Slow down the time factor so the color drift is perceptible day-to-day or hour-to-hour
    # but varying enough to notice.
    # Dividing by a large number would make it static.
    # We want it to be different every time we hit enter.
    offset = (now % 1000) * 0.1 
    print("\n" + print_banner(offset) + "\n")

def run_loop(speed=0.05):
    # Hide cursor
    sys.stdout.write("\033[?25l")
    
    # Reserve space
    dummy_banner = print_banner(0)
    line_count = dummy_banner.count('\n') + 4  # +4 for padding/hint
    print("\n" * line_count) # Make space
    
    offset = 0.0
    try:
        while True:
            # Move cursor up to overwrite
            sys.stdout.write(f"\033[{line_count}A")
            
            # Render
            banner = print_banner(offset)
            hint = f"{C_GREY}Press Ctrl+C to enter the system...{C_RESET}"
            
            # Center the hint roughly
            padding = " " * ((len(BANNER_LINES[0]) - len("Press Ctrl+C to enter the system...")) // 2)
            
            sys.stdout.write("\n" + banner + "\n" + padding + hint + "\n\n")
            sys.stdout.flush()
            
            offset += 0.01
            time.sleep(speed)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        pass
    finally:
        # Show cursor
        sys.stdout.write("\033[?25h")
        # Print a final newline to ensure prompt starts clean
        print()

def handle_sigint(signum, frame):
    sys.stdout.write("\033[?25h") # Show cursor
    sys.exit(0)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, handle_sigint)
    
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="OmegA Sovereign Banner Generator")
    parser.add_argument("--loop", action="store_true", help="Run in an infinite animation loop")
    parser.add_argument("--speed", type=float, default=0.05, help="Animation speed (lower is faster update, default 0.05)")
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    if args.loop:
        run_loop(args.speed)
    else:
        run_once()
